name: demo_button_dialogue
_doc: DEMO A of the Slice-5 interaction demo — BUTTON -> DIALOGUE. An Input SOURCE reads the abstract 'action.interact' key from the per-frame input frame (the F2 portability seam: the room injects action.interact=true the tick the player presses the interact key while targeting the bound object). A Compare gates it (interact truthy, i.e. > 0.5) into a Select that picks the dialogue payload when pressed and an empty payload otherwise. The WorldAction fires dialogue.show with the picked { speaker, text }, which the mounted UI renderer draws. Portable: on a host with no UI surface, dialogue.show falls through WorldActions' unknown-op declared-no-op — the same arrangement runs anywhere. NO new primitive — Input/Const/Compare/Select/WorldAction are all landed.

value.speaker: Terminal
value.text: You pressed interact on the bound object. This dialogue is a dialogue.show receipt rendered in-world.
